# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server';

const AUTH_TOKEN_COOKIE_NAME = 'auth_token'; // Name of the cookie that holds the authentication token

// Routes that are publicly accessible and do not require authentication
const PUBLIC_ROUTES = [
  '/', // Home page
  '/login', // Login page
  '/register', // Registration page
  '/about', // About page
  '/contact', // Contact page
  '/api/login', // API endpoint for logging in
  '/api/register', // API endpoint for registering
];

// Routes that are specifically for authentication (e.g., login/register forms).
// Authenticated users trying to access these will be redirected.
const AUTH_PAGES = ['/login', '/register'];

// Prefixes for routes that require authentication.
// Any path starting with one of these prefixes will be considered protected.
const PROTECTED_PREFIXES = [
  '/dashboard',
  '/profile',
  '/settings',
  '/api/protected', // Example protected API route
];

export function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
  const { pathname } = request.nextUrl;
  const isAuthenticated = request.cookies.has(AUTH_TOKEN_COOKIE_NAME);

  const isPublicPath = PUBLIC_ROUTES.some(path => pathname === path);
  const isAuthPage = AUTH_PAGES.some(path => pathname === path);
  const isProtectedPath = PROTECTED_PREFIXES.some(prefix => pathname.startsWith(prefix));

  // Case 1: User is authenticated
  if (isAuthenticated) {
    // If an authenticated user tries to access login/register pages, redirect them to the dashboard.
    // This prevents logged-in users from seeing the login/register forms.
    if (isAuthPage) {
      return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/dashboard', request.url));
    }
    // For all other routes, allow the authenticated user to proceed.
    return NextResponse.next();
  }

  // Case 2: User is NOT authenticated
  // If an unauthenticated user tries to access a protected path (and it's not also a public path like /api/login)
  if (isProtectedPath && !isPublicPath) {
    // Redirect them to the login page.
    return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', request.url));
  }

  // For unauthenticated users accessing public routes (or any other route not explicitly protected),
  // allow them to proceed.
  return NextResponse.next();
}

// Configuration for the middleware matcher.
// This specifies which paths the middleware should run on.
export const config = {
  matcher: [
    /*
     * Match all request paths except for the ones starting with:
     * - _next/static (static files)
     * - _next/image (image optimization files)
     * - favicon.ico (favicon file)
     * - Any files with common static asset extensions directly accessed (e.g., image, css, js files)
     */
    '/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|.*\\.(?:png|jpg|jpeg|gif|webp|svg|css|js)$).*)',
  ],
};
